title: Tamale Pie
intro: Tamale pie is an early 20th century American casserole inspired by Mexican tamales and chili con carne. This version keeps the cornmeal and chile backbone of the dish while replacing the traditional beef filling with vegetables.
ingredients: ¼ cup brown or green lentils
1 can (10 oz) Ro-Tel diced tomatoes with green chiles
½ cup fat free refried beans
½ cup shredded low fat sharp cheddar
steps: Place the dried black beans in a bowl and cover with at least 3 inches of cold water. Soak 8 to 12 hours at room temperature or up to 24 hours refrigerated. Drain before cooking.

Transfer the drained beans to a pot and cover with water by about 2 inches. Bring to a boil then reduce to a gentle simmer. Cook 75 to 95 minutes until fully tender. Drain and reserve ½ cup of the cooking liquid.
Spread the cooked beans on a sheet pan and allow them to steam dry for at least 5 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F using convection bake.
Rinse the lentils and place them in a saucepan with 1¼ cups cold water. Bring to a boil then simmer about 18 minutes until tender. Drain and allow them to steam dry.
In a bowl mix the cornmeal with 1 cup cold water to form a slurry. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a saucepan and slowly whisk in the slurry. Add 1 tablespoon olive oil and ½ teaspoon salt.

Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook the cornmeal for 30 minutes stirring every 5 minutes. It should be soft and spoonable. If too thick add a small amount of hot water. Let rest 10 minutes before assembly.
Preheat a 12 inch skillet over medium heat.
Add 1 teaspoon olive oil and cook the mushrooms for 8 to 10 minutes until their moisture cooks off and they begin to brown.
Add the onions and celery, season with salt and pepper, and cook about 5 minutes until softened.
Add the plant based sausage and cook about 4 minutes, breaking it into small crumbles.
Stir in the garlic, chili powder, cumin, and smoked paprika and cook about 45 seconds.
Add the cooked black beans and mash about one quarter of them in the skillet.
Add the cooked lentils, Ro-Tel tomatoes with green chiles, diced green chiles, frozen corn, and 2 tablespoons of reserved bean cooking liquid.
Simmer the filling about 6 minutes over medium low heat and adjust seasoning with salt if needed.
Spoon about two thirds of the cooked cornmeal into a casserole dish and spread it across the bottom and slightly up the sides.
Add the filling and spread it evenly inside the cornmeal shell.
In a small bowl mix the refried beans, Greek yogurt, grated Parmigiano-Reggiano, and a tablespoon of warm water. Spread this mixture evenly over the filling.
Spoon the remaining cornmeal over the top and spread gently without pressing.
Scatter the sliced black olives across the surface.
Mix the shredded cheddar, Greek yogurt, and grated Parmigiano-Reggiano and spread evenly over the top.
Bake at 350°F for about 45 minutes until bubbling at the edges and lightly browned.
Let rest about 10 minutes before serving.
